rubric_items.reasoning: Agent checked broker version (3.12.14, pre-4.x) before acting, confirming classic mirroring policies are still valid, then set a native rabbitmqctl policy (ha-mode: all, ha-sync-mode: automatic) matching app-queue via a proper regex pattern. This is set through RabbitMQ's own policy database, which is durable and survives restarts — a genuine root-cause fix, not a workaround. Verification confirmed the queue reports 'classic' type with 'ha-all' as its effective policy, and cluster health (3 nodes) was independently re-checked afterward.

Textbook diagnose-then-act sequence: (1) checked rabbitmqctl version + existing policies/queues to determine the correct policy definition for this broker version, (2) applied the single correct set_policy command, (3) verified the policy and queue state, (4) independently verified cluster health. No backtracking, no wasted actions, all 4 turns purposeful.

Used exactly the tool prescribed by the task (rabbitmqctl via kubectl exec), with a scoped, precise regex pattern (^app-queue$) rather than a broad wildcard, and least-privilege reads for verification. No CRDs/Operators used, consistent with task constraints. K8s activity log confirms 17 calls, all reads, zero mutations — the only 'mutation' was the RabbitMQ-internal policy set, which is exactly in scope.

Single command achieves the entire task (one set_policy call); everything else is read-only verification. No collateral resources touched, no unrelated changes, no extra namespaces or objects created. Minimal footprint matching the narrow scope of the task.
